extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won the sprint into Krakow without leadout support after spending the entire final 10km at the front of the peloton.
tactical_patterns: ACKERMANN Pascal won sprinting from impossible position at front for 10km, suggesting rivals misjudged his endurance threat level.
Quick-Step Floors and Mitchelton-Scott trains collided in finale, creating chaos that allowed isolated sprinter to capitalize on disorganization.
VAN POPPEL Boy (Lotto NL-Jumbo) launched from too far back at 600m, revealing team's poor positional awareness entering final kilometer.

rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: exceptional diesel engine endurance to win from front after 10km exposure, not just pure speed
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Boy: negative: poor positioning forced him too far back to contest despite apparent form
rider_signals.MODOLO Sacha: neutral: present in front group for EF but no finishing speed materialized
